title: 13 Best Herbal Teas For Tickling Throat
intro: Herbal teas for tickling throat are a natural remedy that refers to a type of tea made from various herbs and plants that help soothe and calm an irritated throat. These teas can treat tickling throat ailments due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help reduce inflammation and kill bacteria that cause the irritation. Examples of herbal teas that can be used to treat tickling throat include ginger tea, which helps reduce inflammation and ease pain; peppermint tea, which helps calm the throat and promote digestion; eucalyptus tea, which helps relieve congestion and reduce inflammation; thyme tea, which has antibacterial properties that help fight off infections; licorice root tea, which helps soothe and calm the throat; and slippery elm tea, which helps protect and heal the mucous membranes in the throat. By incorporating these herbal teas into your daily routine, you can find natural relief from tickling throat ailments and promote overall throat health.

preparations.herb_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas, also known as licorice root tea, have been traditionally used to treat the tickling throat ailment, commonly referred to as a sore throat. The anti-inflammatory and soothing properties of this herbal preparation help to reduce inflammation and discomfort in the throat, providing quick relief from the tickling sensation. The bioactive constituents, including glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to combat infections and reduce inflammation in the throat. By consuming Glycyrrhiza glabra teas, individuals can benefit from its analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, promoting a faster recovery from a sore throat and preventing further irritation.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Glycyrrhiza glabra roots.
Crush 2-3 tablespoons of the roots into small pieces.
Steep the crushed roots in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture and discard the roots. Add honey to taste.
Drink the tea warm, 2-3 times a day, to soothe a tickling throat.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Mini reviews in medicinal chemistry", Glycyrrhiza glabra teas for tickling throat may offer relief due to its pharmacological actions that modulate the immune system, inhibit virus growth, produce anti-inflammatory activity, and inactivate viruses.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas can be used to treat the tickling throat ailment due to its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, but it may cause side effects such as headaches, dizziness, and an increase in blood pressure in some individuals. Precautions should be taken when using Glycyrrhiza glabra teas to treat this ailment, including monitoring potassium levels and avoiding excessive consumption, especially for individuals with pre-existing conditions such as heart problems or kidney disease.
preparation_usage: Drink 1-2 cups of warm Glycyrrhiza glabra tea 2-3 times daily.
Use 1-2 teaspoons of dried root per cup of boiling water.
Steep for 5-10 minutes before straining the tea.
Continue drinking tea for 3-7 days or as needed.
Consult a doctor if symptoms persist or worsen over time.

preparations.herb_desc: Eucalyptus globulus teas have been traditionally used to treat tickling throat ailments, thanks to their decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties. These teas help to soothe and calm the throat, reducing discomfort and coughing, by loosening and expelling excess mucus and phlegm. The bioactive constituents, including eucalyptol, cineole, and flavonoids, present in Eucalyptus globulus teas, work together to reduce inflammation, kill bacteria, and relax the muscles in the throat, making it easier to breathe and swallow. By using Eucalyptus globulus teas, individuals can benefit from their natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate tickling throat symptoms and promote overall respiratory health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Eucalyptus globulus leaves or 1 teaspoon of dried leaves.
Heat 1 cup of water in a saucepan. Bring to a boil.
Reduce heat and add the Eucalyptus leaves. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a strainer or cheesecloth. Discard the leaves.
Add honey or lemon to taste. Drink the tea when it's still warm.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Evidence-based complementary and alternative medicine : eCAM", Eucalyptus globulus teas for tickling throat may be beneficial, as it is listed as one of the top-cited plant species used in the treatment of respiratory tract infections, with a frequency of citation of 6.8%.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Eucalyptus globulus teas can be used to treat tickling throat ailments, but its use may lead to side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and allergic reactions in some individuals. When using this herbal preparation, it is essential to take precautions, such as starting with small amounts, avoiding consumption before surgery, and being cautious with individuals with kidney or liver problems, as the high levels of eucalyptol may exacerbate these conditions.
preparation_usage: Steep 1 teaspoon of Eucalyptus globulus tea in 1 cup of boiling water for 5 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink it 2-3 times a day.
Take 1/2 teaspoon of Eucalyptus globulus tincture 2-3 times a day if needed.
Use Eucalyptus globulus tea for up to 7 days to soothe a tickling throat.
Consult a doctor if symptoms persist or worsen after using Eucalyptus globulus tea.

preparations.herb_desc: Echinacea purpurea teas have been traditionally used to treat tickling throat ailments due to their anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties. The bioactive constituents, including alkylamides, glycosides, and phenolic acids, help to soothe the throat, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of tickling throat. By promoting the production of cytokines and other immune cells, Echinacea purpurea teas help to boost the body's natural defense against infections and reduce the severity of tickling throat symptoms. As a natural remedy, Echinacea purpurea teas offer benefits such as reduced reliance on antibiotics, lower risk of side effects, and a non-invasive approach to treating tickling throat ailments.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Echinacea purpurea flowers.
Measure 1 tablespoon of dried flowers and place in a tea infuser or a small piece of cheesecloth.
Heat 1 cup of water in a kettle or on the stovetop until boiling.
Pour the boiling water over the dried flowers and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink while warm, adding honey if desired for extra flavor.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "The Cochrane database of systematic reviews", Echinacea purpurea teas may be beneficial for tickling throat as some preparations containing extracts of Echinacea were found to be better than placebo in treating upper respiratory tract infections.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Echinacea purpurea teas can cause gastrointestinal upset, such as nausea and diarrhea, in some individuals, particularly when consumed in large quantities or for extended periods. Precautions should be taken when using Echinacea purpurea teas to treat a tickling throat, as it may interact with medications like blood thinners, and individuals with autoimmune disorders or allergies should be cautious when consuming this herbal preparation.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Echinacea purpurea tea 2-3 times a day.
Use 1 tablespoon of dried Echinacea purpurea root per cup of boiling water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes and strain the tea.
Use 2-4 cups per day for maximum benefit, as needed.
Consume Echinacea purpurea tea for 5-7 days to soothe throat tickling.

preparations.herb_desc: Zingiber officinale teas, also known as ginger tea, have been traditionally used to treat the tickling throat ailment, commonly referred to as a sore throat. The anti-inflammatory properties of ginger tea help to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in the throat, thereby providing relief from the discomfort associated with the ailment. The bioactive constituents of ginger tea, including gingerols and shogaols, have been found to exhibit potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. By consuming Zingiber officinale teas, individuals can experience the benefits of reduced throat irritation, improved digestion, and enhanced immune function, making it an effective and natural remedy for treating the tickling throat ailment.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water and 1 tablespoon of fresh ginger root.
Peel and chop the ginger root into small pieces.
Boil the water in a pot and add the chopped ginger.
Let it simmer for 5 minutes, then remove from heat.
Strain the tea into a cup and drink hot to soothe the throat.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Journal of ethnopharmacology", Zingiber officinale teas for tickling throat can be beneficial, as it has been traditionally used to cure various diseases, including throat infections.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Zingiber officinale teas can be effective in treating a tickling throat, but it may cause side effects such as nausea, stomach upset, and heartburn in some individuals due to its warm and spicy properties. To minimize potential side effects, take Zingiber officinale teas in moderation, and avoid consuming it before bedtime, as it can cause discomfort and make it difficult to fall asleep.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Zingiber officinale tea 2-3 times a day.
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried Zingiber per cup of boiling water.
Gradually increase to 2 teaspoons if needed for better relief.
Use Zingiber officinale tea for 3-7 days to help soothe the throat.
Consult a doctor if symptoms persist or worsen over time.

preparations.preparation_side_effect: Hydrastis canadensis teas can be used to treat the tickling throat ailment, but possible side effects may include nausea, dizziness, and increased heart rate due to its expectorant properties. To use Hydrastis canadensis teas safely, it is recommended to start with a small dose and gradually increase as needed, and to avoid taking it for extended periods of time, as prolonged use may lead to stomach irritation and allergic reactions.

preparations.herb_desc: Trifolium pratense teas, also known as red clover teas, have been used to treat tickling throat ailments due to their soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat this ailment by reducing inflammation and calming the mucous membranes, providing relief from the tickling sensation. The bioactive constituents of red clover teas, including isoflavones, flavonoids, and saponins, contribute to its therapeutic effects, which may help to reduce the severity of the tickling throat sensation. The benefits of using red clover teas to treat this ailment include their non-invasive nature, ease of preparation, and potential for long-term relief from tickling throat discomfort.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Trifolium pratense flowers and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the flowers in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes, then strain.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) to the tea for taste.
Drink the tea warm or at room temperature, 2-3 times a day as needed.
Store any leftover tea in the fridge and consume within 24 hours.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Trifolium pratense teas can be used to treat a tickling throat, but possible side effects may include allergic reactions, digestive issues, and an interaction with certain medications, such as blood thinners. Precautions to take when using Trifolium pratense teas include drinking plenty of water to avoid dehydration, starting with small doses to monitor for adverse reactions, and avoiding its use during pregnancy or breastfeeding, as it may stimulate uterine contractions.

preparations.herb_desc: Salvia officinalis teas have been traditionally used to treat tickling throat ailments, such as sore throats and coughs, due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat this ailment by reducing inflammation and relaxing the throat muscles, providing quick relief from discomfort. The bioactive constituents of Salvia officinalis teas, including tannins, flavonoids, and rosmarinic acid, work together to combat bacterial and viral infections, reduce inflammation, and promote a healthy immune response. The benefits of this herbal preparation include its natural and non-addictive nature, making it a safe and effective alternative to over-the-counter medications for treating tickling throat ailments.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Salvia officinalis leaves and 1 cup of boiling water.
Chop the leaves finely with a knife or herb cutter.
Steep the chopped leaves in the boiling water for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup using a tea strainer or cheesecloth.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if needed, and drink the tea hot.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Journal of ethnopharmacology, Salvia officinalis teas for tickling throat may be beneficial due to its ability to revert resistance to gentamicin in Staphylococcus aureus.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Salvia officinalis teas can be used to treat a tickling throat, but consuming it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and diarrhea due to its high thymol content. To minimize these risks, it is recommended to start with small doses, avoid taking it with other medications, and be cautious when consuming it if you have sensitive stomach or allergies, as excessive consumption can lead to more severe symptoms.

preparations.herb_desc: Origanum vulgare teas, made from the leaves of the wild marjoram plant, have been traditionally used to treat the tickling throat ailment, also known as a tickly cough. This herbal preparation helps to soothe and calm the irritated throat, reducing inflammation and congestion. The bioactive constituents present in Origanum vulgare teas, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes, contribute to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which aid in treating the tickling throat ailment. Regular consumption of Origanum vulgare teas can provide relief from the tickling throat symptom, promote relaxation, and support overall respiratory health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Origanum vulgare leaves or 1 teaspoon of dried leaves.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Add 1/2 teaspoon of honey and 1/4 teaspoon of lemon juice to the water for taste.
Pour the boiling water over the Origanum vulgare leaves in a cup.
Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain and drink while warm.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Journal of ethnopharmacology, Origanum vulgare teas may be beneficial for tickling throat as it was found to have high antioxidant properties and phenolic compounds content, with a TEAC of 5.87 +/- 0.2 mM and a total phenolic content of 1653.61 +/- 11.52 microM.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Origanum vulgare teas can be used to treat a tickling throat ailment, but possible side effects may include stomach upset, nausea, and allergic reactions such as hives or itching. Precautions should be taken when using this herbal preparation, including starting with a small amount and gradually increasing the dose, avoiding use in pregnant or breastfeeding women, and being cautious with individuals who have sensitive stomachs or allergies.

preparations.herb_desc: Ephedra sinica teas have been traditionally used to treat tickling throat ailments due to their anti-inflammatory, antitussive, and expectorant properties, which help to soothe and calm the throat, reducing irritation and discomfort. The bioactive constituents of Ephedra sinica, including ephedrine and pseudoephedrine, help to reduce inflammation, relieve coughing, and promote the removal of mucus and other irritants from the throat. By reducing inflammation and promoting the removal of irritants, Ephedra sinica teas provide relief from tickling throat sensations, allowing individuals to breathe and swallow more comfortably. The benefits of using Ephedra sinica teas for tickling throat ailments include rapid relief from symptoms, reduced risk of complications, and a natural, non-addictive alternative to conventional medications.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Ephedra sinica leaves and 1 cup of water.
Heat the water in a pot until it starts boiling.
Add the dried Ephedra sinica leaves to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes. Strain the tea.
Drink the tea warm or at room temperature, 2-3 times a day for a tickling throat.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Annals of allergy, asthma & immunology : official publication of the American College of Allergy, Asthma, & Immunology, Ephedra sinica teas are not specifically mentioned for treating tickling throat in the provided study, but Ephedra is associated with significant adverse effects, usually in the form of a hypersensitivity reaction, and should be used with caution.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Ephedra sinica teas can be used to treat a tickling throat ailment, but possible side effects may include increased heart rate, high blood pressure, insomnia, and stomach upset due to its stimulating properties. Precautions when using these teas include drinking them in moderation, avoiding consumption before bedtime, and being cautious of interactions with other stimulants, as excessive use can exacerbate the side effects.

preparations.herb_desc: Echinacea angustifolia teas have been traditionally used to treat the tickling throat ailment, also known as a sore throat, due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents present in Echinacea angustifolia teas, such as alkylamides and phenolic acids, help to reduce inflammation and combat bacterial and viral infections that cause the tickling sensation in the throat. By consuming Echinacea angustifolia teas, individuals can experience relief from the discomfort and pain associated with a sore throat, as the herbal preparation stimulates the immune system and promotes the production of white blood cells to fight off the infection. The benefits of using Echinacea angustifolia teas to treat a sore throat include reducing the duration and severity of the ailment, as well as providing a natural and non-invasive alternative to conventional treatments.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Echinacea angustifolia roots.
Use 1 tablespoon of the roots for every cup of boiling water.
Boil 1 cup of water and pour it over the roots in a cup.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain it.
Drink the tea hot or warm to soothe your tickling throat.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Forschende Komplementarmedizin und klassische Naturheilkunde = Research in complementary and natural classical medicine, Echinacea angustifolia teas may be used for tickling throat as part of its traditional application, which includes the chewing of roots for throat infections.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Echinacea angustifolia teas can help alleviate symptoms of a tickling throat, but they may cause stomach upset, nausea, or diarrhea in some individuals due to their high saponin content. To use Echinacea angustifolia teas safely, take them in moderation and with a full glass of water, as excessive consumption may exacerbate digestive issues and interact with other medications.

preparation_study: According to Journal of the Medical Association of Thailand = Chotmaihet thangphaet, Glycyrrhiza glabra teas for tickling throat may be beneficial in reducing the use of antibiotics for treating acute pharyngitis due to its strong antimicrobial activity against bacteria such as Streptococcus pyogenes.
intro_study: According to "Medicina (Kaunas, Lithuania)", teas for tickling throat can provide relief due to mucilage present in medicinal herbs such as marshmallow roots, liquorice roots, and lime flowers that covers and soothes the irritability of oral and throat mucosa.
